entities.summary: Integer division with ceiling, asserts divisibility

detail.name: divide
detail.description: Utility ensuring tensor dimensions divide evenly for parallel sharding.

teaches.explain: Assert preconditions at boundaries. Fail loudly on invalid input rather than silently producing wrong results.

entities.summary: Abstract base for all linear layer variants

detail.name: LinearBase
detail.description: Defines interface (forward, weight_loader) that all parallel linear classes implement.

teaches.explain: Base class defines skeleton; subclasses override specific steps. Enforces consistent interface while allowing variation.

entities.summary: Configure weight shape and parallelism parameters

detail.name: __init__
detail.description: Sets up weight tensor dimensions accounting for tensor parallel world size.

teaches.explain: Declare shape/config in __init__, but defer heavy allocation (like CUDA tensors) to a later explicit step. Separates config from resource acquisition.

entities.summary: Matrix multiply: output = input @ weight.T

detail.name: forward
detail.description: Core linear transformation. Subclasses may add all-reduce or other communication after this.

teaches.explain: PyTorch modules implement forward() for computation. Called implicitly via module(input). Never call forward() directly.

entities.summary: Standard linear layer replicated identically on all GPUs

detail.name: ReplicatedLinear
detail.description: No sharding — every GPU has the full weight. Used when parallelism is unnecessary for small layers.

teaches.explain: Every device holds identical copy. Simple but wastes memory. Use for small weights where sharding overhead exceeds benefit.

entities.summary: Allocate full (un-sharded) weight tensor

detail.name: __init__
detail.description: Creates weight with full input_size × output_size dimensions.

teaches.explain: Call parent __init__ to reuse base class setup. Subclass only adds what differs.

entities.summary: Load pretrained weights from checkpoint into this layer

detail.name: weight_loader
detail.description: Copy checkpoint tensor directly into module parameter. No reshaping needed for replicated weights.

teaches.explain: Separate weight loading from model construction. Model defines shapes; loader fills values from checkpoint file.

entities.summary: Simple matrix multiply with no communication

detail.name: forward
detail.description: Direct matmul — no all-reduce needed since weight is fully replicated.

teaches.explain: Subclass replaces parent method. Here ReplicatedLinear overrides to skip communication that parallel variants would add.

entities.summary: Linear layer partitioned column-wise for tensor parallelism

detail.name: ColumnParallelLinear
detail.description: Splits weight along output dimension. Each GPU holds W/N columns. Enables model parallelism across GPUs.
detail.why: Models too large for one GPU — split weight matrices across devices

teaches.explain: Split weight matrices across GPUs. Each computes partial result. Combine with all-reduce. Scales to models larger than single GPU memory.
Divide output dimension among devices. Each computes full_input × partial_weight. No communication needed until layer boundary.

detail.design: Column split on first MLP linear enables fused activation without inter-GPU communication
detail.perf: Communication cost is one all-reduce at layer boundary, not inside the matmul

entities.summary: Compute sharded output dimension for this GPU

detail.name: __init__
detail.description: Divides output_size by world_size to determine this device shard size.

teaches.explain: total_size // num_devices gives per-device allocation. Must assert divisibility to avoid shape mismatches.

entities.summary: Load only this GPU shard of the weight from checkpoint

detail.name: weight_loader
detail.description: Slices the full checkpoint weight to extract columns belonging to this device.

teaches.explain: checkpoint_weight[:, shard_start:shard_end] extracts this GPU portion. Avoids loading full weight into GPU memory.

entities.summary: Compute column-parallel matmul (no communication needed)

detail.name: forward
detail.description: Each GPU multiplies full input by its weight shard. Output is partial — communication happens at paired RowParallel layer.

teaches.explain: Delay inter-GPU communication until necessary. Column-parallel output needs no sync until paired with row-parallel layer.

entities.summary: Fuses multiple column-parallel projections into single GEMM

detail.name: MergedColumnParallelLinear
detail.description: Gate and up projections in MLP merged into one weight matrix. Single GEMM then split output by section.

teaches.explain: Combine separate operations into one kernel. Reduces memory bandwidth and kernel launch overhead. Common in inference optimization.

detail.design: gate_proj and up_proj fused — one large GEMM is faster than two small ones on GPU

entities.summary: Configure merged column-parallel with multiple output sections

detail.name: __init__
detail.description: Stores output_sizes list to know where to split after the fused GEMM.

teaches.explain: Store section boundaries so fused output can be split back into logical parts (gate, up) after single matmul.

entities.summary: Load weight shards for each merged section

detail.name: weight_loader
detail.description: Handles loading gate_proj and up_proj weights into correct positions of the fused weight matrix.

teaches.explain: Multiple logical weights stored contiguously in one tensor. Loader must compute correct offset for each section.

entities.summary: Fused Q/K/V projection with per-head tensor-parallel sharding

detail.name: QKVParallelLinear
detail.description: Single matrix produces concatenated Q,K,V. Sharded so each GPU gets complete attention heads.

teaches.explain: Distribute complete attention heads across GPUs. Each GPU computes full attention for its heads without cross-device communication.
Three separate linear projections (Q,K,V) fused into one GEMM for better GPU utilization.

detail.perf: One large GEMM vs three small: better occupancy, fewer kernel launches

entities.summary: Configure Q/K/V dimensions accounting for GQA head counts

detail.name: __init__
detail.description: Handles different num_heads for Q vs K/V (grouped-query attention) when computing shard sizes.

teaches.explain: K/V have fewer heads than Q. Reduces KV cache size by head_ratio×. Each K/V head shared across multiple Q heads.

entities.summary: Load Q, K, V weight shards into fused matrix

detail.name: weight_loader
detail.description: Three-section loader: places Q, K, V weights at correct offsets in the merged weight tensor.

teaches.explain: Fused weight has Q|K|V sections. Loader computes offset per section, handles different shard sizes for GQA.

entities.summary: Linear layer partitioned row-wise for tensor parallelism

detail.name: RowParallelLinear
detail.description: Splits weight along input dimension. Each GPU computes partial output, then all-reduce to sum.

teaches.explain: Collective operation: every device contributes partial result, all receive the global sum. O(data_size) per device regardless of device count.
Divide input dimension. Each device multiplies partial_input × partial_weight. Results summed via all-reduce.

detail.design: Paired with ColumnParallel: Column→activation→Row minimizes total communication

entities.summary: Configure row-parallel with sharded input dimension

detail.name: __init__
detail.description: Divides input_size by world_size for this device row shard.

teaches.explain: Divide along input dimension. Each device holds weight[:, shard_start:shard_end] and receives corresponding input slice.

entities.summary: Load row-sharded weight from checkpoint

detail.name: weight_loader
detail.description: Extracts rows belonging to this device from the full checkpoint weight.

teaches.explain: For row-parallel: slice along dim=1 of checkpoint. Each GPU holds different rows of the weight matrix.

entities.summary: Row-parallel matmul followed by all-reduce

detail.name: forward
detail.description: Computes partial output from this device row shard, then all-reduce sums across all devices.

teaches.explain: After each device computes partial matmul, all-reduce sums results so every device has the complete output. Required for row-parallel.
